stations.name: Morningside railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Within the suburb. Most streets are within a 10 to 15 minute walk.

trains.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 12 to 16 minutes to Central station off-peak.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Morningside via Wynnum Road and Lytton Road.

driving.motorway_access: Story Bridge for the CBD. Gateway Motorway via the nearby Cannon Hill or Lytton interchanges.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 9 to 13 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

connections: Walking access from many streets to the Wynnum Road village strip

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: The Wynnum Road village strip runs through the suburb with cafes, restaurants, and casual dining. Daily-life retail at the Cannon Hill K-Mart Plaza and Bulimba Plaza, both within five to seven minutes.

pockets.name: Wynnum Road village
pockets.character: Streets close to the Wynnum Road shopping and dining strip. Mix of character homes, contemporary builds, and townhouses. Walkability is the proposition.

pockets.name: Station-adjacent
pockets.character: Streets within walking distance of Morningside station. Transport-focused buyer pocket.

pockets.name: Family streets
pockets.character: Quieter residential streets with established family-home stock. Larger blocks and the suburb's traditional character home market.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers moving from Cannon Hill, Murarrie, or further east who want the village strip and train station combination Morningside offers.
Parents targeting the Morningside catchment and the family-suburb feel of the quieter residential streets.
Buyers priced out of Bulimba and Hawthorne who want the inner-east lifestyle at a more accessible entry point.
Train station, village strip, and arterial access support rental demand.
